author: DualyzeAI
description: Create AI Briefs, AI MOCs, Context Packs, Knowledge Books, and Notion Workspaces from your Obsidian notes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: dualyzeAI
manifest.description: Turn your notes into AI-ready context packs. Export clean Markdown for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, NotebookLM, and more. Built-in freshness tracking keeps AI knowledge up to date.
manifest.name: AI Context Pack

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New in v4.1.0

### Notion Workspace Export
- Export entire workspaces as Notion-ready ZIP files
- Includes AI Briefs, AI MOCs, source notes, and images
- Obsidian syntax converted for Notion (wikilinks, embed images, frontmatter stripped)
- URL images kept as-is; local images copied to assets/
- Notion Workspace ZIP tracking in AI Workspace View (5/5 outputs)
- Open folder button for Notion Workspace ZIP row
- Command palette: Export workspace to Notion ZIP

### Improved
- AI Workspace View output count updated to 5
- Progress bar reflects all 5 output types

See [changelog](https://github.com/dualyze-ai/obsidian-context-pack/blob/main/docs/changelog.md) for full history.
releases.name: v4.1.0 — Notion Workspace Export

releases.name: v4.0.2 — Bug fixes

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

Resolves warnings flagged in the Obsidian Community Plugin review.

- Use `window.clearTimeout()` / `window.setTimeout()` instead of global `clearTimeout()` / `setTimeout()` for popout window compatibility
- Fix `Promise returned where void was expected` in EPUB button click handler
- Replace `as any` cast with properly typed internal plugin access to eliminate unsafe-assignment / unsafe-member-access / unsafe-call warnings
releases.name: v4.0.1 — Bug fixes

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## AI Workspace View

v4.0.0 introduces the **AI Workspace View** — a single dashboard to manage AI-ready outputs for each folder in your vault.

### What's New

- **AI Workspace View** — Track AI Brief, AI MOC, Context Pack, and Knowledge Book status per folder
- **Progress tracking** — See completion percentage and output status at a glance
- **Generate / Refresh Workspace** — Generate AI Brief + AI MOC in one click; refresh when notes change
- **Export Pack & Create EPUB** — Launch exports directly from the Workspace View
- **i18n support** — UI text externalized via `t()` for future localization
- **EPUB bug fix** — AI Brief search now uses the same 2-step lookup (frontmatter → naming convention) as workspace state

### Quick Workflow

1. Create a Workspace
2. Add a Folder Workspace
3. Analyze Notes (AI Brief + AI MOC)
4. Generate Knowledge Outputs
5. Export for AI Tools
6. Read as a Knowledge Book (EPUB)

### Tests Added

- `workspaceState.test.ts`
- `epubSanitizer.test.ts`
- `briefExporter.test.ts`
- `types.test.ts`
releases.name: v4.0.0 — AI Workspace View

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## Fixes

- Use Obsidian's built-in `requestUrl` instead of `fetch` for image downloading
- Use `window.setTimeout` / `window.clearTimeout` for popout window compatibility
- Fix TypeScript unsafe-assignment warnings in EPUB renderer

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## What's New

### Knowledge Books (EPUB) — Image Embedding

- Images from notes are now downloaded and embedded directly into the EPUB
- Cover page automatically uses the first image from the notes
- If an image cannot be downloaded, a URL link is shown as fallback
- Supports JPEG, PNG, GIF, SVG, WebP formats

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- `epubBuilder.ts`: Fix unsafe return type (`no-unsafe-return`)
- `epubSanitizer.ts`: Replace literal BOM character in regex with `﻿`
- `epubTemplates.ts`: Remove unused `title` variable in `buildNavXhtml`
- `main.ts`: Remove unnecessary `as ArrayBuffer` type assertion
releases.name: v3.3.1: Fix lint warnings in EPUB modules

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## ✨ Knowledge Books (EPUB)

AI Context Pack can now transform AI Briefs into structured EPUB Knowledge Books.

### Features

- AI-generated book structure from AI Brief
- Cover page with book title
- Overview page with auto-generated intro text from topic names
- AI Brief preface
- Hierarchical table of contents (Cover → Overview → Preface → Topic clusters → Notes)
- Chapter sort order setting (AI Brief order / current / title / filename)
- Kindle-compatible EPUB output

### How to use

Right-click any AI Brief file → **Create Knowledge Book (EPUB)**

Read your Obsidian knowledge in Kindle, Apple Books, Kobo, and other EPUB readers while preserving the structure discovered by AI Brief.
releases.name: v3.3.0: Knowledge Books (EPUB)

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## Changes in v3.2.2

- **Tutorials**: Added step-by-step tutorials for the complete AI Brief → AI MOC → Context Pack workflow
  - [How Alice Turned 20 Art Notes into an AI Knowledge Base](https://github.com/dualyze-ai/obsidian-context-pack/blob/main/docs/tutorials/tutorial-en-paintings.md)
  - [Japanese Tutorial (Art Sample)](https://github.com/dualyze-ai/obsidian-context-pack/blob/main/docs/tutorials/tutorial-ja-paintings.md)
- **README**: Added tutorial links near GIF and in Documentation section
- **Fix**: Remove unused `noteMap` variable in cluster analyzer
- **Fix**: Remove deprecated `setDynamicTooltip()` calls
- **Updated**: package.json description to reflect current plugin capabilities
releases.name: v3.2.2 — Tutorials, README improvements, lint fixes

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## Fixes in v3.2.1

- **Fix**: Cross-language note resolution — when creating a Context Pack from an AI Brief MOC, notes now resolve correctly to the source folder. Prevents Japanese notes from appearing in English packs in mixed-language vaults.
- **Fix**: Remove unused `noteMap` variable in cluster analyzer.
- **Fix**: Remove deprecated `setDynamicTooltip()` calls (2 occurrences) in settings.
releases.name: v3.2.1 — Bug fixes and lint cleanup

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## What's new in v3.2.0

### AI Brief Workflow

AI Context Pack now supports a complete 5-step workflow:

```
Folder
↓
Generate AI Brief
↓
Visualize Knowledge Map
↓
Generate AI MOC from AI Brief
↓
Create Context Pack
↓
Ask questions in ChatGPT / Claude / Gemini / NotebookLM / Claude Code
```

### Changes

- **Generate AI MOC from AI Brief** — new command to transform AI Brief analysis into a navigable AI MOC
- **Knowledge Map** — added to primary README workflow; now a first-class step between AI Brief and AI MOC
- **Mermaid fix** — "Enable Mermaid diagrams" setting now correctly controls output in AI MOC generation
- **Context Pack format** — AI Brief-derived packs use a cleaner format (topic title + description, no tool-artifact headers)
- **AI Brief context menu** — AI Brief files now show dedicated actions (Generate AI MOC, Create Context Pack)
- **Better separation** — AI Brief files are treated as analysis artifacts, not direct Context Pack sources
- Updated README screenshots with Art History example workflow
releases.name: v3.2.0 — Analyze → Organize → Export → Ask

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## AI Brief Generator

Analyze your vault and automatically generate a high-level diagnostic report.

### What's new

- **Topic Clustering** — automatically groups notes by folder, tag, or link patterns
- **Knowledge Map** — Mermaid graph with color-coded clusters
- **Relationship Analysis** — finds similar and related notes within clusters
- **Knowledge Health** — connectivity score, topic coverage score, orphan detection
- **Diagnostic Summary** — natural-language interpretation of vault structure
- **Expansion Candidates** — notes frequently referenced but with thin content
- **Document Structure Mode** — specialized view for single-document vaults
- **Open Questions** — structural gaps and areas for development
- **Suggested AI Prompts** — ready-to-use prompts based on vault content
- **English / Japanese** — full i18n support

### How to use

Right-click any folder → **Generate AI Brief**
releases.name: v3.1.0 — AI Brief Generator

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## Project Knowledge Packs

Track freshness of AI project knowledge directly in Obsidian.

### What's new

- **Freshness panel** — sidebar panel shows all exported packs with 🟢 Fresh / 🟡 Needs Review / 🔴 Stale status
- **Rename tracking** — pack registry auto-updates when notes or folders are renamed or moved
- **Re-export** — one-click re-export with original output target pre-filled
- **Vault-only output** — Context Pack, AI MOC, and MOC files save to configured vault folder
- **i18n** — panel fully localized (English / Japanese)
- **boxes icon** — new ribbon and panel tab icon

See [full changelog](https://github.com/dualyze-ai/obsidian-context-pack/blob/main/docs/changelog.md) for details.

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## What's new

- **Output Target TAB menu** — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Agents tabs replace the single dropdown. Each tab shows context-appropriate options (Chat / Projects / Project / Notebook / Claude Code / NotebookLM).
- **Project Knowledge Instructions** — when Projects, Project, Notebook, or Claude Code is selected, a tailored instruction block is automatically prepended, telling the AI to treat the pack as project knowledge.
- **Token counter update** — estimated token count now includes the Project Knowledge Instructions contribution.

## Also in this release

- README restructured — shorter main page with links to [Features](docs/features.md), [AI Guides](docs/ai-guides.md), and [Changelog](docs/changelog.md)
- GIFs refreshed with new screenshots

releases.name: v2.5.1

releases.author: kojiman55
releases.description:
## What's new

### AI MOC — Generate a Map of Content from any note

Pick any note as a starting point, and AI MOC traces its `[[wikilinks]]` outward to build a structured Map of Content automatically.

**Core Concepts** — notes directly linked from your root note
**Related Notes** — notes linked from *those* notes (depth 2)
**Referenced By** — notes that link back to your root

Optionally generate a Context Pack from all collected notes in the same step.

**Triggers:**
- Right-click any `.md` file → **Create AI MOC from this note**
- Ribbon → **Create MOC (from note)**
- Command Palette → **Create AI MOC from note**

### Other changes

- **Common Instructions** — added a line to prevent AI assistants from offering meta-commentary about Obsidian, Context Pack, or NotebookLM, so responses focus on your actual content
- **Sample vaults updated** — paintings topic added (19 notes each, 86 total); wikilinks and hub notes added throughout recipes and travel notes
- **Screenshots** — all screenshots retaken; demo video added

## Sample vaults

| | Download |
|---|---|
| 🇺🇸 English (86 notes) | [vault-sample-en.zip](https://s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/assets.dualyzeai.com/obsidian-context-pack/vault-sample-en.zip) |
| 🇯🇵 Japanese（86件） | [vault-sample-jp.zip](https://s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/assets.dualyzeai.com/obsidian-context-pack/vault-sample-jp.zip) |
releases.name: v2.5.0 — AI MOC

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### AI-specific starter prompts
Each AI target now receives optimized instructions tailored to its strengths:

- **ChatGPT** — structured responses: headings, bullet points, conclusions first
- **Claude** — focus on relationships, contradictions, gaps, preconditions, and implicit knowledge
- **Gemini** — integrate information across notes; organize by common points, differences, and chronology
- **Claude Code** — treat the pack as project knowledge; follow coding conventions; ask before making assumptions

### Common Instructions
The base prompt is now a shared, editable foundation called **Common Instructions**. AI-specific instructions are appended automatically on export — no manual editing needed per AI.

- Settings: **Common Instructions** replaces "Starter prompt"
- **Reset to default** button added to restore the original base text
- Existing custom prompts continue to work as the common base

### Claude Code
Claude Code now includes AI-specific instructions by default (previously none were added).

### Internal: Agent context
A new `outputKind` field (`chat` / `agent`) distinguishes conversational AI targets from agent-based tools, laying the groundwork for future Cursor and Gemini CLI support.

## Upgrade notes

No migration required. If you had a custom starter prompt set, it will continue to be used as the Common Instructions base. AI-specific additions will be appended automatically on top of it.
releases.name: v2.4.0 — AI-Specific Starter Prompts
